title: Here Comes The Judge

jokes.text: I got my sights set on this school... with the largest repository of girls with the big old butts. Oh, yeah!
jokes.setup: Carlton discusses Princeton's classical literature repository
jokes.punchline: Will mirrors the structure but focuses on girls with big butts instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast between Carlton's pretentiousness and Will's street sensibilities. Strong parallel structure elevates basic crude humor.

jokes.text: 'cause my CD player has the wires on the inside.
jokes.setup: Jazz brings back Will's CD player
jokes.punchline: Will points out the obvious flaw with understated delivery
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - maximum funny per word. The deadpan delivery of an obvious truth creates the humor.

jokes.text: Why would I want to buy a broken CD player?
jokes.setup: Will suggests Jazz should pay for the broken CD player
jokes.punchline: Jazz's illogical response missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Classic Jazz logic - completely missing the moral/financial responsibility angle. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I don't think you'd have to announce it, Mr. Banks. If you ran, we'd hear you coming.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions he hasn't announced running for judge yet
jokes.punchline: Will plays on 'running' meaning both campaigning and physical running, referencing Phil's weight
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect timing. The pun works on multiple levels and flows naturally from the setup.

jokes.text: He still could use the exercise.
jokes.setup: Phil announces he's running for Superior Court Judge
jokes.punchline: Will continues the weight-based humor from the previous joke
jokes.explanation: Immediate callback that extends the running joke but doesn't add much new comedic value.

jokes.text: Whoa, Uncle Phil, you sure you wouldn't want Carlton to wear Aunt Viv's apron?
jokes.setup: Phil describes formal attire for Carlton to wear
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Carlton would look more feminine in an apron
jokes.explanation: Plays on Carlton's preppy, non-masculine image. Character-specific but relies on gender stereotypes.

jokes.text: You told me to get it cleaned. So I washed it.
jokes.setup: Will asks what Jazz did to his jacket
jokes.punchline: Jazz's overly literal interpretation of 'getting it cleaned'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz character moment - literal-minded but well-meaning stupidity. The logic is flawed but understandable.

jokes.text: You make a doorknob look smart, man.
jokes.setup: Jazz has ruined Will's CD player and jacket
jokes.punchline: Creative insult comparing Jazz's intelligence to an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with strong quotability. The comparison to a doorknob is unexpected and visual.

jokes.text: At least I got out all the A.1. sauce.
jokes.setup: Will criticizes Jazz for ruining his jacket
jokes.punchline: Jazz finds a silver lining in an absurd detail
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity of A.1. sauce creates the humor. Jazz's misplaced pride in solving a problem he created is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Will, I hardly know you.
jokes.setup: Will admits he once preferred Yogi Bear cartoons
jokes.punchline: Carlton's shocked reaction to Will showing any intellectual preference
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Carlton's surprise at Will's hidden depths, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Hey, have you been undressing my Teddy Ruxpin again?
jokes.setup: Phil enters looking for Will
jokes.punchline: Unexpected accusation about someone undressing a children's toy
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and absurd line that reveals Phil's childish side. High quotability due to sheer weirdness.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm happy that this fits over my belly.
jokes.setup: Vivian tells Phil to change clothes to project the right image
jokes.punchline: Phil's low bar for clothing success
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about Phil's weight. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Call a cop.
jokes.setup: Ashley complains that Phil forcing her to wear something isn't fair
jokes.punchline: Phil's dismissive response suggesting she should call law enforcement about parenting
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Classic parental dismissal elevated by deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: I've been waiting all my life to hear you say that.
jokes.setup: Phil says 'Well, I'm ready'
jokes.punchline: Vivian's romantic interpretation before Phil clarifies he meant the press conference
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection setup with marital humor payoff. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Thank you. See, Will, I inspire him.
jokes.setup: Phil says Vivian opening her mouth reminds him why he wants to be a judge
jokes.punchline: Vivian completely misinterprets the insult as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Vivian's confidence and ability to spin negatives into positives.

jokes.text: Here, Carlton. Eventually, you'll grow into it.
jokes.setup: Carlton dramatically exits
jokes.punchline: Will's dismissive gesture presumably throwing something at Carlton with a height joke
jokes.explanation: Standard height joke about Carlton with physical comedy element. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Well, down the toilet it goes.
jokes.setup: Phil says his day has been wonderful
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's matter-of-fact prediction that he'll ruin it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery with excellent economy. His pessimistic worldview is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Did I leave here with my pants on? Yes. Oh, thank God. But that means you must throw a lousy party.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson can't remember a recent dinner
jokes.punchline: His relief about keeping his pants on leads to insulting Phil's hosting
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and misdirection. The judge's logic is perfectly absurd - good party means pants come off. High quotability and rewatch value.

jokes.text: Judge, we discussed that over dinner. I'm running against you. What? How dare you! Get out of my house! Out! Lionel, show him the door.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson asks Phil for help with his opponent
jokes.punchline: Phil reveals he IS the opponent, leading to the judge's confused outrage and calling for Lionel
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing the judge's senility. The absurdity of asking to be thrown out of someone else's house by a non-existent person is excellent.

jokes.text: Yes, yes!
jokes.setup: Phil asks if voters want six more years of plea-bargaining
jokes.punchline: Judge Robertson enthusiastically agrees with the criticism of his own record
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing the judge's confusion. He's so senile he agrees with attacks on his own record.

jokes.text: You idiot, you don't even deserve to sit on the bench.
jokes.setup: Phil is giving his campaign speech
jokes.punchline: Judge Robertson suddenly attacks Phil during Phil's own press conference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the judge's erratic behavior at the worst possible moment for Phil.

jokes.text: I didn't know he had kids. He doesn't. And that's not his wife.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson appears with a family
jokes.punchline: Revelation that the family is completely fake
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of political absurdity. The revelation builds the running gag of the judge's questionable tactics.

jokes.text: And am I crazy or does he look 10 years younger?
jokes.setup: The fake family revelation
jokes.punchline: Will notices the judge has somehow gotten younger too
jokes.explanation: Adds to the absurd transformation, suggesting even more artificial enhancement beyond the fake family.

jokes.text: Lovely family. Thank you. Who are they? Rented.
jokes.setup: Reporter compliments the judge's family
jokes.punchline: Judge matter-of-factly admits they're rented
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of an absurd admission. Maximum economy with 'Rented.' Outstanding quotability and the casual admission makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: No, Rahjim Shabaz, Muslim activist. Salaam alaikum.
jokes.setup: Police ask if he's Will Smith
jokes.punchline: Will's instant fake identity with greeting
jokes.explanation: Quick thinking from Will showing street smarts. The fake name and greeting add authenticity to the deception.

jokes.text: And you think that just 'cause you got that badge... and all your tough cop friends walking around... and your cute little walkie-talkie... that you can talk to folks any old way you want to do it.
jokes.setup: Will is told to sit down by the cop
jokes.punchline: Will's building rant that includes 'cute little walkie-talkie'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's attitude and fearlessness. The 'cute little walkie-talkie' detail elevates it from standard confrontation.

jokes.text: Hip-hop cop. Blue looks nice on you, too. Match that big blue vein throbbing in your neck right now.
jokes.setup: The cop is getting angry at Will's attitude
jokes.punchline: Will's observation about the cop's visible anger with color coordination joke
jokes.explanation: Clever visual observation turned into mockery. Will's fearlessness in the face of authority is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: One hell of a Friday night.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil lists Jazz's hangouts from the parking tickets
jokes.punchline: Will's understated assessment of the evening implied by the locations
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with excellent economy. Will's street sensibilities showing through even in trouble.

jokes.text: How could he go to Alice's All Nude Review without me?
jokes.setup: Will realizes Jazz went to these places with his car
jokes.punchline: Will's priorities - more upset about missing out than the tickets
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will's skewed priorities. Even in trouble, he's thinking about what he missed.

jokes.text: I thought he was selling condoms.
jokes.setup: Will approaches the judge asking for help with tickets
jokes.punchline: Judge's complete misunderstanding of the situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing the judge's senility. The specificity of 'condoms' makes it funnier than generic confusion.

jokes.text: You're dead meat, Tiny.
jokes.setup: Judge Robertson reveals his political strategy
jokes.punchline: The elderly judge calling the large Phil 'Tiny'
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic nickname joke. Works because of the size contrast but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: I'd die if you made your own bed.
jokes.setup: Hilary says she'd die if they touched her shoes
jokes.punchline: Phil's dig at Hilary's laziness
jokes.explanation: Good comeback with perfect economy. Plays on the family dynamic and Hilary's spoiled nature.

jokes.text: I'd die if you made your own bed
jokes.setup: Hilary saying she'd die if they touched her shoes
jokes.punchline: Phil's comeback about Hilary's laziness/privilege
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback highlighting Hilary's entitled lifestyle - well-crafted character-based insult

jokes.text: Will, you seem embittered. Jail will do that to a man. Now that you've done hard time, a book may be in order. Criminals sell. I can sell criminals. You're a criminal.
jokes.setup: Will complains about his bad day
jokes.punchline: Carlton's rapid business pitch escalation ending with blunt criminal label
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - turning Will's troubles into a business opportunity with escalating enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Really? And you're too short to ride the teacup ride at Disneyland.
jokes.setup: Carlton calls Will a criminal
jokes.punchline: Will's comeback about Carlton's height with specific reference
jokes.explanation: Good specific comeback. The teacup ride detail makes it more visual and memorable than generic height insults.

jokes.text: Haagen-Dazs opened in Bel-Air?
jokes.setup: Will says Jazz ruined the most important day in Uncle Phil's life
jokes.punchline: Jazz's completely wrong guess about what that day was
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz moment showing his shallow priorities and complete misunderstanding. The specificity of Haagen-Dazs makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Will, I got $2,000 worth of parking tickets.
jokes.setup: Will says Jazz should have told him about the tickets
jokes.punchline: Jazz tells him right now as if that solves the timing problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz logic - technically fulfilling the request while missing the point entirely.

jokes.text: Can I borrow $2,000?
jokes.setup: Jazz promises to take care of the parking ticket problem
jokes.punchline: His solution is to borrow money from Will
jokes.explanation: Classic Jazz non-solution. Perfect economy and timing - the audacity of asking the victim to pay is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Ain't you going to even bother to throw me out?
jokes.setup: Will tells Jazz to leave and that he's not worth throwing out
jokes.punchline: Jazz seems disappointed by the lack of his traditional physical ejection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke acknowledging the show's running gag. Jazz is almost disappointed by not getting his traditional send-off. Excellent self-awareness.

jokes.text: So what are you wearing?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey answers the phone saying it's his accountant
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually a phone sex line
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing Geoffrey's secret life. Perfect economy and timing, though relies on somewhat cheap sexual humor.

jokes.text: Do I look like Jimmie Walker?
jokes.setup: Will invites Carlton to get ribs
jokes.punchline: Carlton's reference rejecting stereotypical Black behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's rejection of stereotypes, though the reference may not land with all audiences.

jokes.text: No, Will, I don't want ribs, I don't want to wax your ride... and I don't even know what a Chumpy is.
jokes.setup: Will approaches Hilary for hanging out
jokes.punchline: Hilary's preemptive rejection ending with confusion about 'Chumpy'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Hilary's complete disconnection from Will's world. The Chumpy confusion adds specificity.

jokes.text: Actually, Aunt Viv, you got a, kind of a... homicidal, sumo-wrestler thing going.
jokes.setup: Phil compliments Vivian's appearance in the newspaper photo
jokes.punchline: Will's more honest assessment with specific imagery
jokes.explanation: Creative and specific visual description. The combination of 'homicidal' and 'sumo-wrestler' creates a vivid and funny image.

jokes.text: What am I, Freud? Grab a fork, Vivian.
jokes.setup: Phil wonders why Jazz brought bean pie
jokes.punchline: Phil's dismissal of psychoanalysis in favor of eating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil pragmatism - dismissing complex psychology for simple pleasure. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: You probably want to hang out with educated people... who drink their malt liquor in a glass.
jokes.setup: Jazz feels inferior about Will going to college
jokes.punchline: Jazz's idea of sophisticated behavior is drinking malt liquor from a glass
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz moment showing his warped understanding of class and sophistication. The specificity of malt liquor in a glass is brilliantly character-specific.

jokes.text: I think you mean hand to mouth, Jazz.
jokes.setup: Jazz says he'll be living 'foot to mouth'
jokes.punchline: Will corrects the malapropism
jokes.explanation: Standard malapropism correction. Jazz's mistake is funny but Will's correction is just functional.

jokes.text: Don't try to impress me with your fancy education.
jokes.setup: Will corrects Jazz's phrase
jokes.punchline: Jazz treats basic English as showing off
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jazz's insecurity about education. Even basic corrections feel like intellectual intimidation to him.

jokes.text: You used to think that was cute.
jokes.setup: Will says Jazz isn't making sense
jokes.punchline: Jazz's nostalgic appeal to when Will found his nonsense endearing
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny moment showing Jazz's awareness of his own shortcomings and his fear of losing Will's friendship.

jokes.text: You used to think that was cute
jokes.setup: Will says Jazz isn't making sense
jokes.punchline: Jazz's nostalgic reference to when his nonsense was endearing
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment - Jazz recognizing how their dynamic has changed

jokes.text: I'm not a faucet to be turned on and off at your whim. I have emotions.
jokes.setup: Will tries to comfort Jazz
jokes.punchline: Jazz's overly dramatic declaration of his emotional complexity
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Jazz trying to be deep while still being fundamentally Jazz. The faucet metaphor is unexpected from him.

jokes.text: I'm not a faucet to be turned on and off at your whim. I have emotions
jokes.setup: Jazz rejecting Will's attempt to comfort him
jokes.punchline: Jazz's dramatic metaphor for feeling manipulated
jokes.explanation: Jazz's surprisingly articulate emotional metaphor - unexpected depth from the character

jokes.text: I said, 'shoot,' Mom.
jokes.setup: Will starts to say something stronger than 'shoot' during the throw
jokes.punchline: Will addresses his mother/the audience about his language
jokes.explanation: Excellent fourth-wall break that acknowledges both Will's character development and the show's family-friendly nature. Meta without being forced.

jokes.text: I raised my boy better than that. Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Will almost curses on TV
jokes.punchline: His mother's disappointed reaction from Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Nice ending callback to Will's mother - brings story full circle with family consequences

tmdb_overview: Will gets arrested for numerous parking tickets as Phil runs for judge against incumbent Judge Robertson.
